name: Database Migrations
description: Safe database migration practices for Prisma, Drizzle, Knex, and raw SQL with rollback strategies
author: StartUpp AI IDE
rules: Every migration must be reversible -- always write both up and down (rollback) logic
Never modify or delete a migration that has already been applied to a shared environment
Add new columns as nullable or with a default value to avoid breaking existing queries during deployment
Split large data migrations from schema migrations; run data migrations as separate scripts
Use transactions for schema changes when the database supports transactional DDL (PostgreSQL, SQLite)
Test migrations against a copy of production data before deploying, not just an empty database
Include a rollback plan in every migration PR description with the exact revert command
Never drop columns or tables directly; deprecate first, then remove in a subsequent release after confirming no code references remain
Use explicit column types and lengths instead of relying on ORM defaults for cross-database compatibility
Name migrations descriptively: YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_add_user_email_index.ts not migration_042.ts

promptTemplates.name: Create Migration
promptTemplates.template: Create a {{orm}} migration that {{migrationDescription}}. Include both up and down functions. Use a transaction where supported. The target database is {{database}}. Consider data preservation and backward compatibility.

promptTemplates.name: Migration Rollback Plan
promptTemplates.description: Generate a rollback strategy for a migration
promptTemplates.template: Create a rollback plan for the following migration: {{migrationDescription}}. Include the down migration code, verification queries to confirm the rollback succeeded, and any data recovery steps needed.

conventions:
Migration conventions:
- Migrations are stored in prisma/migrations/ (Prisma) or db/migrations/ (Drizzle/Knex)
- Each migration is a single file with a timestamp prefix
- Never edit applied migrations; create a new corrective migration instead
- Seed data goes in a separate seed script, not in migrations
- Keep a MIGRATION_LOG.md or use PR descriptions to document what each migration does and why
- Always backup the database before running migrations in production
